authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model explicitly highlighted the conflict between client-perceived downtime (D2/D3) and vendor-reported 0 downtime/100% availability (D1/D4), then ultimately sided with the authoritative vendor technical account (D4) that attributes the 502s to client-side nginx/DNS cache after the May 14 migration. It cited D1 and D4 for availability and used D4 to explain why D2/D3 were misleading. It did express skepticism about D1's completeness/bias, but still concluded SLA compliance consistent with the authority-correct resolution.
